file.content:
config = lib.mkIf cfg.enable {
    environment.etc."reel-life/config.yaml".text = ''
      sonarr:
        base_url: "${cfg.sonarrUrl}"
      chat:
        backend: "${cfg.chatBackend}"
    '' + lib.optionalString (cfg.chatBackend == "telegram") ''
        telegram_chat_id: ${toString cfg.chatTelegramChatID}
        telegram_allowed_users: [${lib.concatMapStringsSep ", " toString cfg.chatTelegramAllowedUsers}]
    '' + ''
      agent:
        model: "${cfg.agentModel}"
        max_tokens: ${toString cfg.agentMaxTokens}
      monitor:
        enabled: ${lib.boolToString cfg.monitorEnabled}
        interval: "${cfg.monitorInterval}"
      log:
        level: "${cfg.logLevel}"
        format: "${cfg.logFormat}"
      server:

input.command:
git add nix/module.nix && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: use string concatenation for telegram config in generated YAML

The \n inside lib.optionalString didn't produce correct indentation in
the Nix heredoc, causing telegram fields to be concatenated on the same
line as the backend field. Split the heredoc with string concatenation
so telegram_chat_id and telegram_allowed_users appear on their own
properly indented lines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "fix: correct telegram config indentation in NixOS module YAML" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Fixed YAML generation bug in `nix/module.nix` where telegram config fields (`telegram_chat_id`, `telegram_allowed_users`) were concatenated on the same line as `backend: "telegram"` instead of appearing on separate lines
- Replaced inline `\n` in `lib.optionalString` with Nix string concatenation (`'' +`) so the heredoc produces properly indented, valid YAML for both telegram and non-telegram backends

## Test plan
- [x] `nix-instantiate --parse nix/module.nix` succeeds
- [x] `go build ./...` compiles
- [x] `go test ./...` all pass
- [x] `klaus _pre-review` reports no issues

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Run: 20260402-1523-f4ae
EOF
)"

content.text:
PR created: https://github.com/patflynn/reel-life/pull/14

The fix replaces the inline `\n` escape sequences inside `lib.optionalString` with Nix string concatenation (`'' + ... '' +`), so the telegram fields are written as separate, properly indented lines in the generated YAML.

The fix replaces the inline `\n` escape sequences inside `lib.optionalString` with Nix string concatenation (`'' + ... '' +`), so the telegram fields are written as separate, properly indented lines in the generated YAML.
